sanskrit:
नष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत।
स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव॥
transliteration:
naṣṭo mohaḥ smṛtir labdhā tvat-prasādān mayācyuta |
sthitosmi gata-sandehaḥ kariṣye vacanaṃ tava ||
literal_meaning: Destroyed is my delusion; memory is regained by Your grace, O Acyuta. I stand firm, doubt gone; I will do Your word.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna reports a complete cognitive-emotional reset: the false self-narrative that paralyzed him has dissolved, autobiographical memory of his sacred duty is restored, and agency is re-anchored in the internalized voice of the divine other (Kṛṣṇa).
psychological_state: Post-therapeutic integration: intrusive doubt (moha) has been replaced by secure-functioning autonomy grounded in an internalized compassionate authority.
trigger_scenario: After months of therapy you suddenly realize the anxious story you've been living—“I must please everyone”—was never yours; you walk out of the session calm, knowing exactly what you have to do even if others disapprove.
anchor_text: Clarity returns when I surrender my story and rise to act on Your word.
